arguments.task: Research Intercom using official first-party sources only. Find and open Intercom developer documentation, authentication docs, REST or GraphQL API reference, rate limit docs, pricing/access pages relevant to API availability, and any official MCP or Model Context Protocol documentation if available. Also check Intercom's official GitHub org for MCP servers if linked from official docs. Extract exact evidence URLs and concise facts for: one-line product description, auth methods supported for API access, whether access is self-serve or gated/paid, API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and any notable signals about buildability. Return the findings with source URLs.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official Intercom pages and gather evidence for API, auth, access, rate limits, and MCP availability.

raw_result.description: AI-powered helpdesk platform with a natively integrated AI agent for customer support.
raw_result.auth_notes: Intercom documents OAuth for public apps and access tokens for private apps created within a workspace.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developer docs and app creation are publicly available; evidence gathered indicates standard API access is self-serve, while some advanced capabilities such as Fin over API and extended API limits are plan- or sales-gated.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad resources such as conversations, contacts, companies, articles, data attributes, plus webhooks, Canvas components, and an official remote MCP server.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Intercom documents an official remote MCP server and an official GitHub repository (intercom-mcp-server); docs note current availability is limited to US-hosted workspaces.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Docs state 10,000 calls per minute per app for private/public apps and 25,000 calls per minute per workspace across all apps; rate-limit headers include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer hub, explicit auth docs, rate-limit docs, broad REST reference, and official MCP documentation/GitHub repo are strong signals. MCP is limited to US-hosted workspaces; pricing evidence also suggests some advanced API-related features are gated separately from baseline API access.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The gathered pricing evidence suggests baseline API access is self-serve, but the exact plan-level availability of all standard API capabilities versus advanced API-related features was not fully verified from a definitive pricing/support statement.

result.description: AI-powered helpdesk platform with a natively integrated AI agent for customer support.
result.auth_notes: Intercom documents OAuth for public apps and access tokens for private apps created within a workspace.
result.gating_reason: Developer docs and app creation are publicly available; evidence gathered indicates standard API access is self-serve, while some advanced capabilities such as Fin over API and extended API limits are plan- or sales-gated.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad resources such as conversations, contacts, companies, articles, data attributes, plus webhooks, Canvas components, and an official remote MCP server.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Intercom documents an official remote MCP server and an official GitHub repository (intercom-mcp-server); docs note current availability is limited to US-hosted workspaces.

result.rate_limit_notes: Docs state 10,000 calls per minute per app for private/public apps and 25,000 calls per minute per workspace across all apps; rate-limit headers include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset.
result.notable_signals: Official developer hub, explicit auth docs, rate-limit docs, broad REST reference, and official MCP documentation/GitHub repo are strong signals. MCP is limited to US-hosted workspaces; pricing evidence also suggests some advanced API-related features are gated separately from baseline API access.
result.human_review_reason: The gathered pricing evidence suggests baseline API access is self-serve, but the exact plan-level availability of all standard API capabilities versus advanced API-related features was not fully verified from a definitive pricing/support statement.
